title: cistus × candidus nothosubsp. candidus
intro: Cistus × candidus nothosubsp. candidus is a nothosubspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, resulting from the cross between Cistus ladanifer and Cistus creticus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects due to the presence of triterpenoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Cistus × candidus nothosubsp. candidus are numerous, including its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Cistus × candidus nothosubsp. candidus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy in traditional medicine.
